label: Set Up Internal Reporting Channel
description: Establish a secure, confidential internal reporting channel that enables both written and oral reporting. The channel must be designed and operated in a way that ensures completeness, integrity, and confidentiality of reports.
steps: Select and configure a reporting channel platform (dedicated hotline, encrypted online form, secure mailbox, or combination)
Ensure the channel accepts written reports (online system, mailbox, or post) and oral reports (phone hotline or voice messaging)
Configure the system to allow in-person meeting requests from reporting persons
Implement access controls so only designated authorised persons can view reports
Test confidentiality and security of the channel before go-live
Document the channel setup and maintain operational procedures

label: Designate an Impartial Person or Department to Handle Reports
description: Designate a specific impartial person or department (internal or external) as competent to receive, acknowledge, follow up on, and maintain confidentiality of reports. Document the designation in writing.
steps: Identify a suitable candidate: compliance officer, legal counsel, HR director, audit committee, or external ombudsman/provider
Verify the designated person/department is impartial and not in a conflict of interest
Formally appoint the person or department in writing
Brief the designated person/department on confidentiality requirements, follow-up procedures, and timelines
Ensure the designated person has authority to escalate and take protective action where needed

label: Communicate Internal Reporting Channel to Workers and Other Persons
description: Make clearly understandable and easily accessible information about the internal reporting channel and procedures available to workers, contractors, suppliers, and other relevant persons.
steps: Draft a whistleblowing policy or information notice covering: how to access the channel, who handles reports, confidentiality guarantees, the right to use external channels, and anti-retaliation protections
Publish the policy on the company intranet and/or internal communications portal
Distribute to all new workers as part of onboarding
Provide information to contractors, subcontractors, suppliers and other work-related persons
Make information available in all relevant languages spoken by workers
Keep the policy current and re-communicate any changes

label: Send Acknowledgement of Report Receipt Within 7 Days
description: Upon receiving a report through the internal channel, send a written acknowledgement of receipt to the reporting person within 7 calendar days, unless they requested no acknowledgement or confidentiality would be jeopardised.
steps: Record the exact date and time the report was received
Assess whether the reporting person has requested no acknowledgement
Assess whether sending acknowledgement would jeopardise the reporting person's confidentiality
Unless exempted, prepare and send written acknowledgement within 7 calendar days
Log the acknowledgement date in the case management system

label: Follow Up on Report and Provide Feedback Within 3 Months
description: Diligently investigate or assess the report and communicate the outcome to the reporting person within 3 months from the acknowledgement date (or 3 months from 7 days after receipt if no acknowledgement was sent).
steps: Assess the report for completeness and plausibility
Determine whether the reported breach falls within the Directive's material scope
Initiate an internal investigation or refer to appropriate body if needed
Document investigation steps and findings
Determine what follow-up action has been or will be taken (investigation, closure, referral to authorities)
Communicate feedback to the reporting person within 3 months, describing actions taken or intended
Log the feedback date in the case management system

label: Implement Confidentiality Protections for the Reporting Person
description: Ensure the reporting person's identity and any information that could identify them is not disclosed without their explicit consent, beyond authorised personnel who need to handle the report.
steps: Restrict access to reports and case files to designated authorised persons only
Implement access logging and audit trails on the reporting system
Train authorised staff on confidentiality obligations and consequences of breach
Where necessary, anonymise the reporting person's identity in communications with departments under investigation
Obtain explicit written consent before disclosing the reporter's identity for investigative purposes
Document any accidental or required disclosures (e.g., to judicial authorities) with justification

label: Maintain Compliant Records of Reports
description: Keep complete records of every report received. For oral reports, create recordings or transcripts. For in-person meetings, create minutes. Retain records only as long as necessary and in compliance with GDPR.
steps: Implement a case management system for recording all reports with metadata (date, channel, subject matter)
For telephone/voice reports: obtain consent to record, or create a written transcript and allow the reporting person to verify and correct it
For in-person meetings: prepare detailed minutes and share with the reporting person for verification and correction
Apply GDPR data retention policies — define and document retention periods
Ensure secure, encrypted storage of all report records
Destroy records after the retention period following data deletion procedures

label: Establish and Enforce Anti-Retaliation Policy
description: Implement organisational policies, training, and monitoring to ensure no retaliation is taken against reporting persons, facilitators, or third parties connected with them.
steps: Draft and adopt an anti-retaliation policy prohibiting all forms of retaliation listed in Article 19
Communicate the policy clearly to all managers and HR personnel
Train managers on identifying and avoiding prohibited retaliatory actions
Establish an internal process for reporting persons who believe retaliation has been taken against them
Monitor HR decisions (promotions, transfers, performance reviews, terminations) for patterns linked to prior reports
Document any reviewed cases and action taken to address retaliation

label: Implement GDPR-Compliant Data Protection for Reporting Channels
description: Ensure all personal data processed through internal reporting channels complies with GDPR requirements, including lawfulness of processing, data minimisation, security, retention, and data subject rights.
steps: Conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for the reporting channel system
Identify and document legal basis for processing under GDPR
Implement technical and organisational security measures (encryption, access controls, audit logs)
Define and document data retention periods for reports and related case files
Establish procedures for handling data subject access requests from reporting persons and concerned persons
Include the reporting channel in the register of processing activities (RoPA)
Update privacy notices to cover whistleblowing processing

label: Competent Authority: Set Up External Reporting Channel
description: Competent authorities designated by the Member State must establish secure, confidential, and operationally independent external reporting channels and publish accessible information about them.
steps: Establish dedicated reporting channels (online portal, hotline, secure mailbox) independent from other authority functions
Ensure the channel allows written and oral reporting and in-person meetings
Publish accessible information on how to report, procedures, timelines, confidentiality protections, and anti-retaliation measures
Train staff on handling reports, maintaining confidentiality, and applicable legal obligations
Establish case management systems and internal procedures for follow-up
Establish referral procedures for reports outside the authority's competence
